items.title: Proposed KWAN laws widen funding base beyond Petronas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The newly-passed National Trust Fund (KWAN) bill is expected to reduce the fund's reliance on Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) by introducing new statutory funding sources while allowing contributions from a wider range of donors. Petronas has contributed a total of RM13.5 billion to the KWAN since its establishment nearly four decades ago. Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the proposed legislation marks a shift from the existing framework, under which Petronas has remained KWAN's sole voluntary contributor since its establishment.

items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: Media centres to open for election coverage from tomorrow
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Three media centres will be opened for the Negri Sembilan election from tomorrow until Aug 1 by the Communications Ministry through the Information Department and the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC). "Media practitioners may use the facilities and access information available at the media centres, while those reporting from other areas can utilise facilities and internet access at 60 Nadi centres throughout Negri Sembilan designated as support media centres," it said. The locations of NADI centres are available at https://www.nadi.my/lokasi-nadi.

items.title: Russia and Ukraine strike vessels in Black Sea, wheat prices jump
items.summary: MOSCOW: Ukraine and Russia launched missile and drone attacks on Thursday on vessels in the Black Sea and the Sea of Azov, stepping up hostilities in a zone vital for grain exports that have triggered a rise in global wheat prices. Ukraine's military said it struck six Russian tankers and two tugboats, while Russia's defence ministry said its forces hit a maritime vessel and a speedboat of the Ukrainian armed forces while they were en route to ports in the Odesa region. Russian forces also struck a number of other military and industrial targets, the ministry said.

items.title: Saarani: Perak unity govt to remain intact until end of term
items.summary: Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said all state executive council (exco) members had reaffirmed their commitment to continue working together until the dissolution of the State Legislative Assembly. He said the coalition, comprising BN and PH, remained stable and was unaffected by DAP's decision to withdraw its support for the Melaka state government. "After DAP announced its withdrawal from the Melaka unity government, I met all my exco members yesterday before the state executive council meeting.

items.title: Chinese banks set for US$41mil payday from chipmaker's US$8.6bil IPO
items.summary: The payout would bring the year's total fees from mainland IPOs to about US$684.62 million, gaining on last year's US$984.75 million, LSEG data showed. China Securities and CICC are sponsoring the IPO, or playing lead roles in the share sale, IPO filings showed. Other banks involved are China Merchants Securities , Guotai Haitong Securities, Guoyuan Securities and Huatai Securities unit Huatai United Securities, the filings showed.

items.title: Two Hong Kong drug arrests raise concerns over recruitment of Malaysian drug mules
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two Malaysians have been arrested in separate drug trafficking cases at Hong Kong International Airport within three days, as authorities believe international syndicates continue recruiting drug mules from Malaysia. Hong Kong Customs said officers discovered the suspected cocaine concealed inside the shoes worn by the passenger during customs clearance. Hong Kong Customs found 3kg of cannabis buds.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends higher on broad-based buying
items.summary: Among heavyweights, Maybank jumped eight sen to RM11.04 and Public Bank rose 15 sen to RM5.20. Tenaga Nasional fell eight sen to RM14.30, CIMB was one sen weaker at RM7.70, and IHH Healthcare slipped nine sen to RM8.53. As for the active stocks, Tanco added half a sen to 32 sen, Nationgate soared 20 sen to RM1.17, VS Industry perked up 1.5 sen to 23.5 sen, SFP Tech rose four sen to 34 sen, while HHRG slid two sen to 14.5 sen. Of the top gainers, Nestle went up RM1.00 to RM93.00, United Plantations climbed 30 sen to RM35.00, Critical Holdings surged 24 sen to RM1.90, and Fraser & Neave put on 18 sen to RM29.28.

items.title: MCMC orders harmful online content blocked within 24 hours
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Communications Ministry, through the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), has made it mandatory for licensed service providers to block high-priority harmful online content within 24 hours. The measure, enforced through the Online Safety (Period) Regulations 2025 under the Online Safety Act 2025 (Act 866), aims to curb the spread of fake news, disinformation, and misinformation at an early stage. According to the ministry, from Jan 1, 2022, to May 31, 2026, MCMC submitted 63,215 requests to service providers to remove false content, resulting in the successful takedown of 54,626 pieces of content, or 86 per cent.

items.title: Network School probe: No evidence yet of Israelis in programme, says Immigration
items.summary: Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said investigations into the allegations were ongoing, but no substantial evidence had been found so far. He said several government agencies were also conducting investigations within their respective jurisdictions. Zakaria said the department would continue its investigations and share its findings with the relevant agencies.

items.title: PH ready for any BN-PN election strategy in Negri Sembilan, says Loke
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Pakatan Harapan is prepared for any political strategy by its rivals, including possible cooperation between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional, ahead of the Negri Sembilan state election. DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said that, based on previous political developments, including the Johor election, PH would not be unsettled by any political manoeuvres by its opponents and would instead remain focused on strengthening its own election machinery. He said PH's priority at present is to reinforce unity among its component parties to secure victory in the contested seats.

items.title: Court of Appeal upholds 'Mentega Terbang' film ban
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Court of Appeal today upheld the government's prohibition on the screening, distribution, and promotion of the film "Mentega Terbang". A three-member bench comprising Justices Datuk Ismail Brahim, Datuk Ong Chee Kwan, and Datuk Ahmad Shahrir Mohd Salleh unanimously dismissed an appeal by the film's director, Mohd Khairianwar Jailani, and producer, Tan Meng Kheng. Delivering the unanimous decision, Justice Ong said the home minister had lawfully exercised his powers under Section 26 of the Film Censorship Act 2002 (FCA) in issuing the prohibition order.

items.title: Padini says MACC has unfrozen all bank accounts linked to probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Padini Holdings Berhad said today that the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has unfrozen all of the company’s and its subsidiaries’ bank accounts that had been frozen as part of an anti-money laundering investigation, adding that no one from the group has been arrested or charged. In a public statement, the retailer said all affected accounts had been released after being frozen since April. “The Company is pleased to announce that all affected bank accounts of the Company and the Group have been unfrozen by the MACC,” it said.

items.title: Malaysia is fifth largest source of foreign investment in Indonesia's Q2
items.summary: JAKARTA: Malaysia was the fifth-largest source of foreign investment in Indonesia in the second quarter (Q2) of 2026, contributing US$700 million, while Indonesia's realised investment totalled Rp511.8 trillion. Investment and Downstreaming Minister Rosan Roeslani said Hong Kong was its largest source of foreign investment in Q2 at US$5.5 billion, followed by Singapore (US$4.2 billion), China (US$1.7 billion) and Japan (US$900 million). Singapore remained as Indonesia's largest foreign investor in H1 2026, with total realised investment of US$8.8 billion, followed by Hong Kong (US$7.8 billion), China (US$3.9 billion), Japan (US$1.9 billion) and the United States (US$1.7 billion), he said.

items.title: UST Malaysia chief recognised with Penang state award
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: UST Malaysia vice president and country head Amar Chhajer has been awarded the Darjah Johan Negeri (DJN) by Penang Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib. This marks Chhajer's second state recognition from Penang, following the Pingat Jasa Kebaktian awarded to him in 2020. Chhajer, who serves as vice president and country head of UST Malaysia, oversees the company's business growth across Southeast Asia.

items.title: Perak unity govt remains stable despite political tensions elsewhere, says Saarani
items.summary: IPOH, July 16 — Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad today said the state’s unity government remains stable and will not be affected by political developments in other states that have led to tensions within governing coalitions. Saarani stressed that Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders in Perak remain committed to serving the people and ensuring the state administration continues to function smoothly. He said the situation in Perak differs from developments elsewhere, including in Melaka, where DAP recently announced its withdrawal from the state unity government.

items.title: Sara hits 99pct utilisation rate, RM3.45 billion spent
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (Sara) programme recorded a 99 per cent utilisation rate among nine million monthly Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) recipients, with spending reaching RM3.45 billion this year. The Finance Ministry said under the Sara Untuk Semua initiative, some 22 million people, or 87 per cent of all recipients, generated over RM1.77 billion in local market transactions. "Recipient spending performance is a key gauge of how effectively STR and Sara are easing cost-of-living pressures for low- and middle-income groups.

items.title: Govt weighs SST relief for elderly care centres amid cost concerns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — The Ministry of Finance (MoF), together with the Ministry of Women, Family and Community Development (KPWKM), is conducting a further study on a proposal to exempt elderly care centres from the Sales and Service Tax (SST). Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the study includes a review of the existing service tax to identify categories of care centres providing basic or premium services. “Any proposals for improvements from all stakeholders will also be taken into consideration before the study is finalised,” he said during the Dewan Rakyat Special Chamber session today.

items.title: Penang State Museum to reopen on July 23 after nearly a decade of restoration
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 16 — The Penang State Museum on Farquhar Street, which has been closed for restoration works since 2017, will finally reopen next week. State Tourism and Creative Economy Committee chairman Wong Hon Wai said restoration works on the heritage building have finally been completed. Restoration works at Fort Cornwallis, including the reconstruction of the moat, began around 2022.

items.title: All 16 state education departments to host MSSM events this year
items.summary: BANGI: All 16 state education departments, including those in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan, will host Malaysian Schools Sports Council (MSSM) events for the first time this year. Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the milestone was made possible through close collaboration between the Education Ministry, state education departments and district education offices, allowing wider participation in school sports nationwide. "Earlier, I asked (Education Ministry Sports, Co-curriculum and Arts Division director) Cikgu Ithnin what was special about this year's MSSM.

items.title: Asian LNG prices surge to highest since late March amid Hormuz supply disruption
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Asian liquefied natural gas (LNG) prices climbed to their highest level since late March as renewed hostilities around the Strait of Hormuz disrupted supplies from the Persian Gulf and prompted buyers to seek replacement cargoes, Anadolu Ajansi reported. The Japan-Korea Marker, the benchmark for spot LNG deliveries to Northeast Asia, rose 19.66 per cent on Wednesday to US$19.93 per million British thermal units. Fresh hostilities in and around the Strait of Hormuz have restricted LNG shipments from the Persian Gulf, which accounts for roughly 20 per cent of global supply.

items.title: SARA aid sees strong uptake with 99pc utilisation among STR recipients
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — The Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA) programme has seen a 99 per cent utilisation rate among nearly nine million monthly Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) recipients, with spending hitting RM3.45 billion so far this year. The Ministry of Finance (MOF) also reported that under the SARA Untuk Semua initiative, some 22 million people, or 87 per cent of all recipients, have generated over RM1.77 billion in local market transactions. “Recipient spending performance is a key gauge of how effectively STR and SARA are easing cost-of-living pressures for low- and middle-income groups.

items.title: Traffic cop jailed four months, fined RM1,000 for bribery
items.summary: KUANTAN: A traffic police corporal was sentenced to four months' jail by the Sessions Court here today after being found guilty on four counts of accepting bribes amounting to RM1,000. Judge Sazlina Safie made the decision after finding that the defence had failed to raise reasonable doubt against the case faced by Ahmad Amin Khairul Komar, 42, at the end of the defence case. "After examining the entirety of the evidence from both the prosecution and the defence, the court decides that at the end of this defence case, the prosecution has successfully proven the case against the accused person beyond reasonable doubt.

items.title: Psychiatrist highlights complexity of suicide risk factors in Zara inquest
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A child's perception of a positive environment and interactions is important when assessing suicide risk factors, rather than focusing solely on parental separation and conflict, a psychiatrist told the coroner's court. Consultant psychiatrist Dr Wong Haw Huo told the court that clinicians had seen many cases involving children whose parents were not separated but who were still associated with suicidal behaviour. "In reality, as clinicians, we see many patients whose parents were not separated, yet their children were still suicidal.

items.title: ESSZone gets security upgrade with new patrol boats, radar systems and surveillance assets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16— The government is continuing to strengthen security in the Eastern Sabah Security Zone (ESSZone) through an integrated approach involving federal security agencies, including the addition of strategic assets, the modernisation of operational capabilities and enhancements to surveillance systems. Shamsul Anuar said the government is also implementing an asset modernisation programme for the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), involving the Marine Police Force, General Operations Force (GOF), Special Task Force, VAT 69 Commando and the Special Actions Unit (UTK). For the Marine Police Force, several new assets are being acquired, including a Police Transport Vessel (PTV) for Lahad Datu, which is currently under procurement, while two patrol vessels in Sandakan and Lahad Datu are undergoing ground refit works.

items.title: Johor to produce AI cleaning robots under China partnership
items.summary: KULAI: Johor is set to become the production base for artificial intelligence (AI)-powered cleaning robots under a technology transfer partnership between a local company and a Chinese robotics firm. Indah Holding Venture (M) Sdn Bhd director Datuk B Ravintharan said the company plans to manufacture the robots at its Senai factory for export and the domestic market. Ravintharan said the collaboration followed an introduction by the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida) to a Chinese robotics company seeking a local partner.

items.title: High Court awards RM40,000 to man who sued JPJ D-G over ‘MADANI’ number plate proposal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — The High Court has ordered the Road Transport Department (JPJ) director-general to pay RM40,000 as compensation and RM25,000 in costs to Malaysian man Hasan Azhari Hashim for infringing the latter’s copyright on a proposal to issue “MADANI” as special vehicle number plates. On January 15, 2025, Hasan Azhari had sued the JPJ director-general over the latter’s launch of public bidding for the “MADANI” special number plates. Hasan Azhari claimed that the JPJ director-general had copied his idea and concept in his copyrighted proposal for “MADANI” number plates.

items.title: Traffic corporal gets four-month jail term for taking bribes through wife’s account
items.summary: KUANTAN, July 16 — A traffic police personnel with the rank of corporal was sentenced to four months’ jail by the Sessions Court here today, after being found guilty on four counts of accepting bribes amounting to RM1,000. Judge Sazlina Safie made the decision after finding that the defence had failed to raise reasonable doubt against the case faced by Ahmad Amin Khairul Komar, 42, at the end of the defence case. “After examining the entirety of the evidence from both the prosecution and the defence, the court decides that at the end of this defence case, the prosecution has successfully proven the case against the ACC (Accused Person) beyond reasonable doubt.

items.title: No roadblocks but enforcement remains, police tell RXZ Members 8.0 riders
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 16 — Terengganu police will take firm action against any RXZ Members 8.0 participant found violating the law or road regulations. Its chief Datuk Mohd Khairi Khairudin said that although no roadblocks would be mounted, police would continue monitoring the situation and carry out enforcement through patrols and planned operations. He said no exemptions or special treatment would be accorded to RXZ Members 8.0 participants, and they would be issued summonses or face stern action if found committing offences.

items.title: Father recalls son's final wishes after fatal school bus tragedy
items.summary: KLUANG: A 35-year-old father shared his profound grief after his 7-year-old son, Muhammad Umar, died after being hit by a school bus in Taman Sri Lalang yesterday afternoon. Mohd Faaiz Muran said he was at work in Selangor when he was informed of the 4.30pm accident and rushed back immediately. I could not bear to look at his condition," Faaiz said when met after his son's funeral at the Sri Lalang Islamic Cemetery today.

items.title: Mindef says Constitution gives Armed Forces authority over military exercises
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Defence Minister Datuk Seri Khaled Nordin said in Parliament today that he agrees with the argument that military exercises conducted in Pahang do not require consent. Ikmal said this power is clearly outlined in the Federal Constitution, to which Khaled agreed. Among the explanations in his own question, he said the Federal Constitution clearly outlines Federal and State powers,” Khaled said.

items.title: Discussions with other coalitions had my approval as PN chairman, Samsuri tells Muhyiddin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: All discussions and engagements with outside parties ahead of the Negri Sembilan election were conducted with the knowledge and permission of Perikatan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar. In response to allegations made yesterday by Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, Samsuri said these discussions have since been finalised and approved by the PN supreme council at today's meeting. Yesterday, Muhyiddin expressed disappointment with PN component party Pas for negotiating with Barisan Nasional without involving other parties in PN.

items.title: KWAN law closes loophole exposed by 2021 RM5bil withdrawal
items.summary: Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the 2021 episode, which drew public attention, highlighted three fundamental weaknesses in the 1988 National Trust Fund Act. Liew said under the new Bill, any withdrawal exceeding the annual limit – capped at half of the fund's expected long-term real rate of return – would require a Dewan Rakyat resolution. The MACC Act 2009 would also apply.

items.title: Kota Damansara residents take objections over proposed data centre project online
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kota Damansara residents have raised objections to a proposed commercial data centre in their neighbourhood, citing concerns over traffic congestion, road safety, environmental impact and the project's proximity to residential areas. Residents took to social media yesterday to voice their concerns, with many questioning the suitability of locating a data centre within a residential neighbourhood. "Data centres don't belong in residential areas.

items.title: Germany's Delivery Hero agrees €12.7bil takeover by Uber
items.summary: FRANKFURT: German food delivery group Delivery Hero said Thursday it has agreed to be taken over by US ride-hailing giant Uber in a €12.7 billion (US$14.6 billion) deal. Uber is offering €41.50 per share for Delivery Hero, valuing the deal at €12.7 billion. "Uber's global mobility and delivery platform and our shared commitment to innovation make this the right partnership to build on Delivery Hero's strengths in local food delivery and quick commerce," said Niklas Oestberg, chief executive officer (CEO) and co-founder of Delivery Hero.

items.title: Two debt collectors jailed 18 months, fined RM20k for harassing borrowers
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Two debt collectors were sentenced to 18 months' jail and fined RM20,000 each by the magistrate's court today after pleading guilty to harassing and threatening borrowers in March and April. For the first charge, Noor Farah Hazwani sentenced each accused to eight months' jail and a RM10,000 fine, in default of three months' imprisonment. For the second charge, both men were fined RM10,000 and sentenced to 10 months' jail each.

items.title: #NSTviral: 'Crab-walking' bus sparks road safety concerns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A bus that appeared to move sideways "like a crab" while travelling forward has sparked concern among social media users over its roadworthiness. A Threads user, @izmieunifi, shared a video of the bus and questioned how a vehicle in such a condition could still be allowed to operate on the road. https://www.threads.com/@izmieunifi/post/Da0JoOglF_L?xmt=AQG0Iyne4uZ26e3enTEibnt_Dmkw8psPo_MnysCsskVqLp9-7kP0ulEeOnGN419F6dFJYOuS&source_surface=35&slof=1 The post drew numerous reactions from other Threads users, many of whom expressed concern over the bus' unusual movement.

items.title: Standing tall for 17 years, 'IPOH' sign ready for a makeover
items.summary: IPOH: After standing tall for 17 years as one of the city's most recognisable landmarks, the iconic "IPOH" sign along Jalan Kuala Kangsar near Gunung Lang has been taken down for restoration works after showing signs of deterioration. The Ipoh City Council (MBI) in a statement said this was the first time the sign has been taken down since it was installed in 2009. It added that the restoration works on the sign overlooking Gunung Lang began on Monday, with the refurbishment expected to be completed next month.

items.title: Press freedom row: Media groups condemn alleged curbs at Ayob Khan event
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two media associations have condemned what they described as unnecessary restrictions imposed on journalists and photographers during an event attended by Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay today. The Selangor Photographers and Journalists Association (Wajar) and the Crime Journalists Association (CiJA), in a joint statement, alleged that media photographers were barred from taking photographs and told to rely solely on official images provided by police. Wajar and CiJA called on Bukit Aman and the Selangor police contingent headquarters to clarify whether the restrictions were imposed on Ayob's instructions.

items.title: Cultivating visionary policymakers in maritime education
items.summary: Thus, maritime education must evolve beyond producing technically competent seafarers and marine professionals, given what has happened in maritime hotspots in the Middle East which is reshaping global geopolitics and geoeconomics. Technical expertise remains essential, but it must be complemented by policy knowledge to ensure sustainable, secure, and resilient maritime development. Decisions by governments and international organisations directly influence shipping operations, port management and maritime safety.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng urges Penang to defer water tariff increase
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Bagan Member of Parliament Lim Guan Eng has urged the Penang government to postpone a planned 20 sen per cubic metre water tariff increase for one year. Lim said the proposed increase, from the current average tariff of 62 sen per cubic metre, would affect households and businesses already facing higher costs. State Infrastructure, Transport and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said households using 35 cubic metres of water a month would see an increase of up to RM2.55 under the new tariffs.

items.title: National Trust Fund Bill 2026 passed with tighter rules on contributions, withdrawals and governance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — The Dewan Rakyat today passed the National Trust Fund (KWAN) Bill 2026 which strengthens the legal basis of the fund through more consistent inflows, more disciplined disbursements and clearer governance and accountability. The bill, tabled by Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong, was passed with a majority of votes in favour after being debated by 15 Members of Parliament in the Dewan Rakyat today. “This bill is enacted to strengthen the framework through statutory contributions, withdrawal discipline and modern governance,” said Liew.

items.title: Zara Qairina’s early months at boarding school marked by stress, peer tensions, inquest told
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 16 — The Coroner’s Court here today heard that Zara Qairina Mahathir experienced difficulties adjusting to secondary boarding school, particularly during her first few months there. Referring to a report jointly prepared with Prof Dr Aili Hanim Hashim, Dr Wong said Zara Qairina appeared to struggle with peer relationships and experienced tensions with schoolmates during her early months at the boarding school. After reviewing the available evidence, Dr Wong described Zara Qairina as a vulnerable adolescent who shared a close, loving and trusting relationship with her mother.

items.title: KL’s new LaLaport transport hub targets 10,000 passengers a day, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Kuala Lumpur’s newest integrated transport hub is targeting up to 10,000 passengers a day as it strengthens connections between rail, buses and other transport services while serving as a key gateway for travellers to and from Singapore, Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh said today. Launching the LaLaport Transportation Hub, Yeoh said Kuala Lumpur sees 1.2 million people entering and leaving the city daily, with about 5.5 million journeys recorded each day, making investments in urban mobility increasingly important. “The LaLaport Transportation Hub is a welcomed addition to Kuala Lumpur’s transport network.

items.title: Court of Appeal upholds ‘Mentega Terbang’ ban, rules film censorship powers constitutional
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Malaysia’s Court of Appeal has upheld the government’s ban on the controversial film Mentega Terbang , ruling that the home minister’s power to prohibit films in the public interest is constitutional and may be exercised before any public disorder occurs. A three-member bench comprising Justices Datuk Ismail Brahim, Datuk Ong Chee Kwan and Datuk Ahmad Shahrir Salleh unanimously dismissed an appeal by the film’s director, Khairi Anwar Jailani, and producer, Tan Meng Kheng, affirming the High Court’s dismissal of their judicial review application, Free Malaysia Today (FMT) reported. Delivering the court’s broad grounds, Justice Ong rejected the appellants’ argument that Section 26 of the Film Censorship Act, which empowers the home minister to prohibit a film in the “public interest”, is unconstitutional.

items.title: Malaysia condemns Israel’s latest settlement expansion in Occupied Palestinian Territory
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Malaysia strongly condemns Israel’s latest announcement of a US$2.3 billion settlement expansion plan in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, calling the plan a deliberate attempt to entrench the Zionist regime’s illegal occupation and undermine the inalienable rights of the Palestinian people. “Such actions demonstrate Israel’s continued disregard for its obligations under international law, including relevant United Nations Security Council resolutions and the Fourth Geneva Convention. Malaysia said the regime’s continued settlement expansion cannot be justified under any pretext whatsoever.

items.title: Supermarket worker charged with murdering newborn baby in Subang Jaya
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 16 — A supermarket sales assistant was charged at the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of her newborn baby girl at an apartment in Subang Jaya last week. No plea was recorded from Siti Nur Wahida Zainudin, 23, after the charge was read out before Magistrate Ameera Mastura Khamis, as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court. The woman, who appeared in court wearing a dark blue long-sleeved T-shirt and black trousers, is alleged to have murdered the baby girl she had given birth to between 5am and 5.30pm on July 7, 2026, at an apartment unit in USJ 14, Persiaran Mulia, Subang Jaya.

items.title: Search launched for fisherman feared overboard off Labuan
items.summary: Labuan police chief Supt Wan Mohd Firdaus Wan Zaki said police received a report on the incident at 10.20 am from the victim’s employer after the vessel’s captain informed them that one of the crew members had gone missing while fishing. “Preliminary investigations found that the victim had deployed fishing nets using a small boat before he went missing. Wan Mohd Firdaus said crew members conducted an initial search around the area but failed to locate the fisherman before lodging a police report.

items.title: Fifty ‘sick’ projects nationwide under close monitoring, says Works Ministry
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 16 — The Works Ministry (KKR) is closely monitoring 50 “sick” projects nationwide to ensure their implementation returns to schedule, Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi said. He said the 50 delayed projects were identified from a total of 865 projects under the ministry’s purview, with only seven of the 104 KKR projects in Kelantan classified as “sick”. “To date, we have identified 50 sick projects out of 865 projects nationwide.

items.title: Samsuri rebuts Muhyiddin, says PAS-BN talks had his approval
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar today rejected Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s claim that PAS acted unilaterally in engaging with Barisan Nasional (BN) ahead of the Negeri Sembilan state election, saying all preliminary discussions were conducted with his knowledge and approval. “These matters were subsequently finalised and approved by the PN supreme council at today’s meeting.” The statement directly contradicted Muhyiddin’s assertion yesterday that PAS had held discussions with BN without consulting PN’s other component parties or obtaining approval from the coalition’s supreme council. Samsuri also said the PN supreme council had approved the allocation of Negeri Sembilan state seats among PAS, Wawasan, Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People’s Party (MIPP).

items.title: Tapped just twice in 40 years, Putrajaya tables Bill to tighten National Trust Fund withdrawals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Malaysia has tapped its RM22.43 billion National Trust Fund (KWAN) only twice in nearly four decades, including to fund Covid-19 vaccines, Putrajaya told Parliament today as the government moved to tighten withdrawal rules. The Dewan Rakyat this afternoon debated the National Trust Fund Bill 2026, which Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said aims to overhaul the management of the fund, including introducing an annual spending cap. “This record reflects the original intention behind the establishment of KWAN as a long-term national savings fund, to be used only when absolutely necessary,” Liew said.

items.title: ‘They’re working tirelessly’: Resident says Sandakan’s Duchess of Kent Hospital, not its staff, needs fixing
items.summary: SANDAKAN, July 16 — The condition of Duchess of Kent Hospital Sandakan has once again come under the spotlight after a Sandakan resident shared concerns on social media over the state of the hospital’s facilities, equipment and infrastructure. The condition of one of the sinks at the hospital. Based on visits to Lahad Datu Hospital, Tawau Hospital and hospitals in Kota Kinabalu, the resident felt that those facilities appeared more modern than Duchess of Kent Hospital.

items.title: Sabah police appeal for witnesses after nine China tourists injured in head-on crash involving tour van and Perodua Axia
items.summary: BEAUFORT, July 16 — Nine China tourists were injured when their van collided with a Perodua Axia at Km 21 of the Beaufort-Papar Road, near Kampung Gana, Membakut, on Tuesday. The Axia driver was transported to hospital by passing motorists before firefighters arrived while the van passengers were taken to Beaufort Hospital by ambulances and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) vehicle. The Axia driver, Javidah Osman, 35, suffered minor injuries, while the tourist van driver, Muhammad Farah Izyan, 26, was seriously injured.

items.title: Miri woman loses RM1.39m after clicking fake cake ad on social media
items.summary: MIRI, July 16 — A 43-year-old woman lost RM1,389,132 after falling victim to an elaborate scam that began with an online cake advertisement on social media. Miri police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) received a report on Wednesday on unauthorised withdrawals from the victim’s company bank accounts. After checking with the bank at around 10am on Wednesday, she discovered that 19 unauthorised fund transfers had been made from two of her company bank accounts to 15 different bank accounts the previous day, resulting in losses totalling RM1,389,132.

items.title: Govt to pilot affordable MediAsas health insurance plan this month ahead of nationwide rollout in 2027, says deputy minister
items.summary: Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the pilot programme would pave the way for the plan’s full implementation nationwide in January 2027. This will allow more Malaysians to access private healthcare protection,” he said during the question-and-answer session. He said BNM would introduce a no look-back policy under the MediAsas plan to ensure a fairer insurance market.

items.title: More than 3,600 Sabah citizenship applications still pending, Dewan Rakyat told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — As of May 31 this year, 3,640 citizenship applications in Sabah were still pending, while 10 had been approved and citizenship certificates issued to the successful applicants. Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said that, for late birth registration applications in Sabah, 2,659 applications had been approved, while 611 were still being processed. He said the Home Ministry (KDN) remained committed to expediting decisions on citizenship applications, particularly those involving late birth registration, through improvements to procedures, shorter waiting times and greater transparency in providing information to applicants.

items.title: Muhyiddin’s trial: Company’s RM800,000 donation to Bersatu linked to Jana Wibawa project, witness says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — KCJ Engineering Sdn Bhd’s RM800,000 donation in 2022 to political party Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia is related to a government project it had won under the Jana Wibawa programme, the High Court heard today. KCJ’s finance manager Zuriedah Deres@Musa said this while testifying as the 19th prosecution witness in former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s power abuse and money laundering trial in relation to Jana Wibawa. Today, Zuriedah said KCJ’s managing director Datuk Azman Yusoff had on September 1, 2022 instructed her to prepare the company’s cheques to donate RM800,000 to Bersatu.

items.title: Nearly 70pc of road accident victims last year were aged 16 to 40, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — A total of 69.4 per cent of all road accidents in the country last year involved people aged between 16 and 40. Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said there were 6,157 cases involving individuals aged 16 to 20, followed by 5,978 cases involving those aged 21 to 25, 4,716 cases involving those aged 26 to 30, and 3,640 cases involving individuals aged 31 to 35. “Heavy vehicles, drunk driving and reckless driving are among the main factors contributing to the high number of road accidents in the country,” he said during the oral question-and-answer session in the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: Religious affairs minister: Govt strengthens crackdown on online deviant teachings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — The government is adopting a whole-of-government approach to strengthen cooperation among agencies in efforts to contain the spread of deviant teachings, which are increasingly circulating on digital platforms and across borders. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs), Zulkifli Hasan, said the government, together with the Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (JAKIM) and state Islamic religious authorities, remains vigilant in monitoring and addressing any deviant doctrines that go against the Ahli Sunnah Wal Jamaah school of thought. Zulkifli said the coordinated efforts of multiple agencies, including JAKIM, state Islamic religious departments, police, the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission, the National Security Council, local authorities, the Immigration Department and the National Registration Department, had enabled several enforcement actions.

items.title: Loke: Pakatan unfazed by possible BN-Perikatan cooperation in Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 16 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) says it is prepared for any political strategy by its rivals, including possible cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN), ahead of the Negeri Sembilan state election. DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said that, based on previous political developments, including during the Johor state election, PH would not be unsettled by any political manoeuvres by its opponents and would instead remain focused on strengthening its own election machinery. Whatever the challenges and competition, ultimately it is up to the people to decide,” he told reporters after officiating the 2026 Kampung Angkat Madani (Madani Adopted Village), Sekolah Angkat Madani (Madani Adopted School) and Santuni Madani programme organised by the Ministry of Transport in Kampung Mantin Dalam, near here, today.

items.title: Eleven held, including employer, after foreign worker dies from head injuries in Johor Bahru
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 16 — Johor police have arrested 11 suspects, including the victim’s employer, over the alleged murder of a foreign worker who was found covered in blood on a sidewalk in Taman Universiti here before dying in hospital days later. Johor Bahru North police chief Assistant Commissioner Radin Ramlan Radin Taha said authorities received a call from the public about the incident at 2.27am on June 28. “Following that, the victim was taken to Sultanah Aminah Hospital (HSA) Johor Bahru for treatment,” he said in a statement.
